created_for: GS LinkOps AI resource database building

records.name: Rocket Lab announced acquisition of Iridium Communications
records.structured_summary: Rocket Lab announced an approximately USD 8 billion agreement to acquire Iridium Communications. The transaction would combine launch, spacecraft systems and an operational L-band LEO communications constellation, including globally coordinated spectrum and an established enterprise and government customer base. Closing is expected in 2027 and remains subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.
database_targets: Supplier Concentration and Vertical Integration Model
records.confidence_level: High for the announced transaction terms from Reuters; ownership change must not be treated as completed until closing is confirmed.
pending_confirmation_fields: post-closing Iridium legal entity and contracting entity
changes to gateway or ground infrastructure ownership
changes to customer agreements and service pricing
records.next_action: Create ownership-event records rather than overwriting the current operator owner. Add effective_from and effective_to dates after the transaction closes.

records.name: Service chaining and virtual network function placement for ground station networks
records.structured_summary: The study models ground-station service processing as a chain of virtual network functions deployed on shared compute and network infrastructure. Satellite mission plans and predictable contact windows are used to place functions and route traffic before execution, with the optimization objective reducing link utilization and server consumption. The architecture supports separating physical antennas from software-defined processing resources.
records.confidence_level: High for the proposed research architecture and optimization formulation; medium for operational applicability because results are simulation-based.
records.next_action: Add processing_chain and compute_resource entities so antenna capability matching also checks whether required demodulation, decoding and delivery functions can be placed during the contact.

records.name: High-altitude ground stations for all-optical LEO constellations
records.structured_summary: The paper proposes high-altitude platform stations acting as elevated optical relay nodes between LEO spacecraft and terrestrial ground infrastructure. The concept reduces the atmospheric path affecting space-to-ground optical links and creates a distinct asset class between spacecraft, conventional optical ground stations and airborne platforms. It is useful for modelling future optical service chains even though it is not yet a mature commercial GSaaS architecture.
database_targets: Relay and Hybrid Link Architecture Library
records.confidence_level: High for the published architecture and simulation concept; low for near-term commercial availability.
records.next_action: Introduce relay_node and delivery_path_hop entities so GS LinkOps AI can represent future non-terrestrial relay assets without misclassifying them as conventional fixed ground stations.
